top_stories.title: BeyondTrust warns of critical flaws in remote access software
top_stories.summary: BeyondTrust disclosed two critical security vulnerabilities in its Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access software products. The flaws could allow attackers to bypass authentication, requiring immediate customer patching.

top_stories.title: BeyondTrust Patches Critical Auth Bypass Flaws in Remote Support and PRA
top_stories.summary: BeyondTrust has released patches for two critical security vulnerabilities affecting its Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access products. The flaws, including CVE-2026-40138 with a CVSS score of 9.2, allow unauthenticated attackers to take control of affected devices.

top_stories.title: Completes Entro Security Acquisition To Strengthen AI Identity Governance
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has completed its acquisition of Entro Security, a move aimed at strengthening AI identity governance and securing AI agents and machine identities. The acquisition follows a series of bullish analyst calls for SailPoint, which is publicly traded on NASDAQ under the ticker SAIL.

top_stories.title: SailPoint (SAIL) Completes Entro Security Acquisition To Strengthen AI Identity Governance
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has completed its acquisition of Entro Security, expanding its identity governance capabilities. The deal adds Entro's non-human identity and secrets security technology to SailPoint's AI-driven identity governance platform.

top_stories.title: Barracuda adds PAM and identity protection with Evo Security acquisition
top_stories.summary: Barracuda Networks has acquired Evo Security to expand its BarracudaONE platform with privileged access management (PAM), access control, identity protection, and identity threat detection and response capabilities. The acquisition integrates Evo Security's identity solutions into Barracuda's existing identity-driven controls to create a unified identity security architecture delivered through a single platform.

news.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign is targeting marketing professionals with the goal of stealing Google account credentials. The attack uses nested redirects and other evasion techniques to bypass detection and compromise accounts.

news.title: Microsoft to enable Windows settings backup by default for orgs
news.summary: Microsoft will enable Windows settings backup and restore by default on Microsoft Entra-joined and hybrid-joined enterprise systems following an upgrade to Windows 11 26H2. The change affects organizations using Microsoft Entra for device identity management.

news.title: DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign dubbed DEBULL has been observed abusing Microsoft's legitimate device-code authentication flow to compromise Microsoft 365 accounts, active between late June and early July 2026. The attack bypasses traditional credential phishing by using collaboration-themed lures to direct victims through the real Microsoft device login experience, as reported by ZeroBEC.

news.title: Court Filing Reveals Windows Device ID Helped FBI Trace Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker
news.summary: A federal complaint reveals that FBI investigators used a persistent Windows device ID to trace an alleged Scattered Spider hacker to a 2025 intrusion at a luxury jewelry retailer. Microsoft records linked the device ID to accounts used to maintain access during the breach, connecting them to 19-year-old suspect Peter Stokes.

news.title: Writer AI Flaw Could Let Agent Previews Leak Session Tokens Across Tenants
news.summary: Researchers disclosed a now-patched session isolation vulnerability in Writer's enterprise AI platform that could allow cross-tenant session token leakage. The flaw, dubbed WriteOut by Sand Security, could enable an unauthenticated attacker to compromise any Writer AI tenant via a one-click attack.

news.title: Your company already adopted AI and nobody is governing access
news.summary: Antoine Berton, CTO at Elba Security, discusses the ungoverned access risks created when employees connect AI tools to corporate systems without oversight. The video focuses on how unsanctioned AI tool adoption creates an unmanaged access surface that falls outside existing identity governance controls.

news.title: Microsoft wants to keep your AI agents from going rogue
news.summary: Microsoft has introduced Microsoft Execution Containers (MXC), a policy-driven execution layer designed to govern and constrain AI agent behavior on Windows and WSL platforms. The technology enforces access controls and execution boundaries for AI agents, directly addressing authorization and access management concerns in agentic AI systems.

news.title: Phishing poses as big-brand job interview to steal Google accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign is impersonating over 30 major brands through fake job interview lures to steal Google account credentials from marketing professionals. The attack targets Google account authentication credentials, making it relevant to identity theft and account compromise vectors.

news.title: What does zero trust mean for mobile devices in the enterprise?
news.summary: The article examines how zero trust security principles apply to mobile devices in enterprise environments. It addresses the challenge of securing corporate resources as mobile device usage grows and traditional perimeter-based defenses prove insufficient.

news.title: Enforce least-privilege authorization in multi-agent AI chains using Cedar
news.summary: The article covers enforcing least-privilege authorization in multi-agent AI systems using Cedar, Amazon's policy language, to prevent unauthorized scope expansion during agent task delegation. The focus is on maintaining proper authorization boundaries across multi-hop agent chains, directly addressing access control and authorization principles in emerging AI architectures.

news.title: ModelCop Launches AI Agent Security Platform, Targets $25B Machine Identity Market
news.summary: ModelCop has commercially launched an identity-first security platform targeting AI agents and Non-Human Identities (NHIs), entering the machine identity market. The platform is aimed at enterprise security teams and focuses on visibility and governance for AI agent identities, addressing a market the company values at $25 billion.

news.title: Backs Q2 Guidance at Investor Day
news.summary: SailPoint confirmed its Q2 adjusted EPS guidance of 7-8 cents against an 8-cent consensus at its Investor Day on June 17, 2026. The company also reaffirmed its Q2 revenue guidance at the same event.

news.title: SailPoint (SAIL) Backs Q2 Guidance at Investor Day
news.summary: SailPoint confirmed its Q2 adjusted guidance at an Investor Day event on June 17, 2026. The announcement was made in the context of SailPoint being highlighted as one of eight high-growth cybersecurity stocks.

news.title: When checking the URL isn’t enough: a Device Code Phishing attack via a Microsoft website
news.summary: The article covers Device Code Phishing attacks that abuse the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow to steal authentication tokens, bypassing traditional phishing defenses. The attack exploits a legitimate Microsoft authentication mechanism, making it particularly dangerous as checking URLs for legitimacy is insufficient to detect the threat.

news.title: Channel moves: Who’s gone where?
news.summary: A weekly roundup of personnel moves covers staffing changes at various technology companies, including One Identity, an IAM vendor. The article is primarily a broad channel industry personnel update spanning multiple unrelated companies.

news.title: OAuth, guest accounts, and weak MFA drive SaaS risk
news.summary: A report identifies OAuth integrations, guest accounts, and weak MFA configurations as primary drivers of SaaS security risk. Guest accounts created for contractors and partners frequently remain active beyond their intended use, creating persistent unauthorized access paths to corporate data.

news.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
news.summary: Researchers at Jamf Threat Labs discovered a new macOS infostealer called PamStealer that exploits PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) checks to steal Mac login passwords. The malware is distributed as a fake Maccy clipboard manager app in the form of a compiled AppleScript file, using PAM system calls to harvest credentials.

news.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
news.summary: A new infostealer dubbed 'Djinn' is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The malware specifically targets credentials that link development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize high-profile accounts, including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official's accounts. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal authentication and account recovery processes were shared on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting Congressional inquiries from both chambers. CISA is still working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files describing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes alongside the compromised credentials.
